content: @Andreas Hindborg I say your OnceLock type that you sent a patch for. Is this type supposed to have a different purpose than OnceLock in the stdlib?
It is similar but with a reduced feature set. It is a container that can be written only once. I believe the std lib one can be reset as well.

Should the method names be aligned with the std OnceLock as much as possible?

The method seem to map this way:

  • new -> new
  • as_ref -> get
  • populate -> set
  • copy -> no obvious equivalent in my head (maybe I am missing it)
I am just talking about method names, not suggesting using the std version. That would make no sense.
Also, in the std version, don't you have to have a mut reference to reset it? Couldn't you prevent that by only passing non-mut reference after initializing it?
Yes, you are right. I should do that. copy is just a short ahnd for my_once_lock.as_ref().map(|v| v.copied())). We could remove it.
Yeah, I think you only need a real OnceLock type. Maybe only implement the non-mut part of the std api until we find some use for them?
Feel free to respond on list. I think it would help the other reviewers see your comments.
Thanks. I just didn't want to waste folk's time. I'll reply on list.
